items.name: Hip Flask
items.examine_text: A dented hip flask. Empty, naturally. Story of my life.

ethel_hs.speaker: player
ethel_hs.text: I'm not offering an empty flask to the filing golem. She'd file a grievance.

gerald_hs.speaker: player
gerald_hs.text: Gerald looks like he could use a drink, but I'm fresh out. Welcome to the club, pal.

water_cooler.speaker: player
water_cooler.text: I briefly consider filling the flask with water. Then I remember I have standards. Low standards, but standards.

noticeboard.speaker: player
noticeboard.text: I can't drink a noticeboard. Believe me, I've tried worse.

chairs.speaker: player
chairs.text: No amount of alcohol would make these chairs comfortable. Not that I have any to test the theory.

desk.speaker: player
desk.text: I already checked the desk. That's where I found this tragically empty flask in the first place.

crystal_ball.speaker: player
crystal_ball.text: I hold the flask up to the crystal ball. It shows me a vision of the flask... still empty. Thanks for nothing.

filing_cabinet.speaker: player
filing_cabinet.text: I don't think the filing cabinet accepts liquid bribes.

pip_hs.speaker: player
pip_hs.text: I hold the filing reference out toward Pip.

pip_hs.speaker: pip
pip_hs.text: GER-LAB-7742/B! I love that you're organising things. Organisation is a form of self-care.

pip_hs.speaker: player
pip_hs.text: I'm going to go use this on the filing cabinet now.

bookshelf.speaker: player
bookshelf.text: Books and booze don't mix. Well, actually they mix brilliantly, but not like this.

wanted_poster.speaker: player
wanted_poster.text: I'm not wasting good— wait, it's empty. Never mind.

hat_stand.speaker: player
hat_stand.text: I briefly consider using the flask as a hat. Things haven't gotten that bad yet. Close, though.

takeaway_containers.speaker: player
takeaway_containers.text: Mixing old kebab grease with an empty flask. Even I'm not that desperate.

pip_hs.speaker: player
pip_hs.text: I hold out the empty flask.

pip_hs.speaker: pip
pip_hs.text: Oh! Are you sharing? That's a real gesture of vulnerability, Mortimer.

items.name: Filing Reference Card
items.examine_text: A small card with 'GER-LAB-7742/B' written on it in impossibly neat handwriting. Below that, in smaller letters: 'Filed under: Labyrinth, Stolen (see also: Minotaur, Distressed)'.

filing_cabinet.speaker: player
filing_cabinet.text: Here we go. One case file, pre-prepared. Ethel is frighteningly efficient.

ethel_hs.speaker: ethel
ethel_hs.text: I gave you that card so you could use the FILING CABINET, Mr. Grimshaw. In your OFFICE. That's the room with the desk and the regrettable smell.

gerald_hs.speaker: player
gerald_hs.text: Waving a filing reference at the client isn't going to help. I need to actually pull the file first.

items.name: Case File
items.examine_text: A manila folder labelled 'GERALD — Labyrinth, Stolen.' Inside: a form in triplicate, a risk assessment, and a surprisingly accurate sketch of a labyrinth. Someone has written 'URGENT — client is a minotaur and keeps pacing' in red ink.

gerald_hs.speaker: player
gerald_hs.text: Right, Gerald. I've got your case file here. Let's make this official.

gerald_hs.speaker: gerald
gerald_hs.text: Oh! Oh, you mean... you'll take my case? Really truly?

gerald_hs.speaker: player
gerald_hs.text: Against my better judgement, yes. Someone's nicked your labyrinth and I'm going to find out who.

gerald_hs.speaker: gerald
gerald_hs.text: Oh thank you, thank you! You won't regret this!

gerald_hs.speaker: player
gerald_hs.text: I already regret this. But that's never stopped me before.

gerald_hs.speaker: player
gerald_hs.text: Right. A stolen labyrinth. How hard can it be to find an entire labyrinth?

ethel_hs.speaker: ethel
ethel_hs.text: That file goes to the CLIENT, Mr. Grimshaw. The large anxious gentleman with the horns. In the WAITING ROOM.

filing_cabinet.speaker: player
filing_cabinet.text: I just took this OUT of the filing cabinet. Putting it back would be the most pointless thing I've done today. And the bar is high.

pip_hs.speaker: player
pip_hs.text: I hold out the case file.

pip_hs.speaker: pip
pip_hs.text: Oh, is that Gerald's case file? I love that you're taking tangible steps. Tangible steps are so grounding.

pip_hs.speaker: player
pip_hs.text: I need to give this to Gerald, Pip. Not to you.

pip_hs.speaker: pip
pip_hs.text: Of course! Go! Take action! I'll be here vibrating with pride.
